votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking that the lengths match and that for every index i, output[i] is the maximum of all elements in input up to index i.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking length equality and verifying that each element in the output is the maximum of the input elements up to that index.

The Python code correctly checks the length equality and iterates through the lists to verify both the upper bound condition and the existence condition for the rolling maximum, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it checks that output length equals input length, that for each position i all elements input[0..i] are <= output[i], and that some element input[j] equals output[i] for j <= i. The range(i+1) correctly covers j from 0 to i inclusive, matching the Coq condition (j <= i)%nat.
